atomic_match_rate: 15/18 pipeline events matched to Alex atomic rows (83%)

actor.note: Target sale rows typically have no bidder actor

actor.pipeline: Party A + Other strategic NDA signers + Financial NDA signers
actor.note: Pipeline splits into grouped actors (11S + 14F = 25); Alex uses aggregate label

field_comparisons.note: No direct Alex match for Party B bidder_interest on 2016-04-21. The export CSV row 2 (Bidder Interest, Party B, S, 2016-04-21) is pipeline-only; Alex has BidderID 2 (G&W, date 2016-04-13) as a different event

round_label.note: Alex labels this 'Final Round Inf Ann' (informal round announcement). Pipeline labels it final_round_ann with round_scope=formal.

disagreements.filing_snippet: representatives of GHF instructed all potential buyers to submit non-binding letters of intent (LOIs) by July 20, 2016, together with mark-ups of the forms of merger agreement
disagreements.arbitration_note: Filing says LOIs with merger-agreement mark-ups were requested -- this is a formal-round signal. Alex's 'Inf' label appears to be a judgment call that the process had informal characteristics, but the structural signal (LOI + mark-up deadline) supports formal.

disagreements.filing_snippet: In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs ... Party B submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $24.00 per share
disagreements.arbitration_note: Filing says 'In late July 2016' for the batch of six LOIs. Alex uses 2016-07-20, which was the LOI deadline date; the LOIs were received after the deadline. Pipeline's 'late July' normalization is more faithful to the text.

disagreements.filing_snippet: representatives of GHF instructed all potential buyers to submit non-binding letters of intent (LOIs) by July 20, 2016 ... Party B submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $24.00 per share (subject to an expedited diligence review)
disagreements.arbitration_note: Filing describes these as 'non-binding letters of intent' which is an informal/non-binding signal. Alex calls Formal because it was submitted in response to a process letter with merger-agreement mark-ups. Party B also provided mark-ups (evident from later merger-agreement negotiation). Pipeline classifies as Informal based on 'non-binding' LOI label. Both interpretations are defensible; this is a definitional gray area where LOIs submitted with mark-ups in response to a formal process letter could go either way.

disagreements.filing_snippet: G&W submitted an LOI on July 21, 2016 to acquire the Company for a price of $21.15 per share ... The price included $20.02 cash at closing and $1.13 in the form of a contingent value right
disagreements.arbitration_note: Filing says $20.02 cash + $1.13 CVR = $21.15. This is technically NOT all-cash since it includes a CVR component. Alex marks all_cash=1, which is questionable. Pipeline has null. Neither is precisely right: the correct value would be 0 (not all-cash) since CVR is not cash. Pipeline's null is less wrong than Alex's 1.

disagreements.filing_snippet: In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs ... Party E (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.26 per share
disagreements.arbitration_note: Same as Party B. Filing says 'late July 2016'. Alex uses the LOI deadline date July 20, but the filing text says the LOIs were received in late July.

disagreements.filing_snippet: In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs ... Party D (a financial buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.00 per share
disagreements.arbitration_note: Same batch. Filing says 'late July 2016'. Pipeline is more faithful.

disagreements.filing_snippet: In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs ... Party C submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $19.30 per share
Party F (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $19.20 per share
Party F (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $19.20 per share
disagreements.arbitration_note: Filing explicitly calls Party F 'a strategic buyer'. Alex codes Party F as financial (F). This is a clear Alex error.

disagreements.filing_snippet: Party E (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI ... Party E submitted a revised LOI, along with financing support, from Party F, at a price of $23.81 per share
disagreements.arbitration_note: Filing calls Party E 'a strategic buyer'. Party F's role here is financing support for Party E's bid. Alex codes Party F as financial (F), creating a S/F mixed label. The filing itself says 'Party F (a strategic buyer)' in the LOI table (BidderID 13 context), so Party F is strategic. Pipeline labels both as strategic. The 'financing support' role does not change Party F's bidder_kind. Pipeline is more consistent with filing text.

disagreements.filing_snippet: G&W submitted a revised LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $25.00 per share in cash, which excluded the previously proposed CVR, together with mark-ups of the merger agreement and the voting agreement. G&W's LOI further indicated that the offer would expire at 6:00 p.m. on the following day.
disagreements.arbitration_note: This revised LOI was submitted with mark-ups of the merger agreement and voting agreement, a binding deadline (expiry at 6pm next day), and was the penultimate step before execution. The formality signals (mark-ups + expiry deadline) strongly suggest Formal. Pipeline's Informal classification appears to be a pipeline error -- the enrichment classified all proposals as Informal based on 'non-binding' LOI label, but this revised LOI with merger-agreement mark-ups and an expiry is functionally a formal/binding offer.

field_mismatches.note: Filing says 'In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs'. Alex uses the deadline date; pipeline uses the filing's explicit time reference.
LOI is labeled 'non-binding' in filing (informal signal) but submitted with merger-agreement mark-ups in response to a formal process letter (formal signal). Definitional gray area.
Revised LOI with merger-agreement mark-ups and 24-hour expiry deadline is functionally formal. Pipeline's blanket Informal classification is a pipeline enrichment gap.
Filing explicitly calls Party F 'a strategic buyer'. Alex error.
Filing identifies both as strategic. Alex appears to infer F from Party F's financing role.
Filing says $20.02 cash + $1.13 CVR. Neither all-cash nor null captures this correctly. Correct answer would be 0 (not all-cash).

pipeline_only.note: Filing says 'on April 21, 2016, the Company and representatives of GHF held an introductory meeting with another potential strategic buyer (Party B)'. Alex does not record this as a separate event. Alex has BidderID 2 for G&W with date 2016-04-13 instead, which is a different event (Connections Convention meetings). Pipeline is filing-grounded.

pipeline_only.summary: G&W indicated possible intention to submit revised LOI
pipeline_only.note: Filing says 'On August 11, 2016, G&W indicated its possible intention to submit a revised LOI on August 12, 2016'. Alex does not capture this as a separate event. Pipeline is filing-grounded.

Filing says 'The Company issued a press release publicly announcing the transaction on Monday, August 15, 2016'. Alex does not have a separate press release row (the Executed row covers it). Pipeline granularity is finer.

alex_only.note: Filing describes Party A suggesting joint ventures and expressing interest in Q4 2015. This is the initial contact event. Pipeline captures Party A's involvement in evt_001 (target_sale) and evt_002 (nda) but does not have a standalone bidder_interest event for Party A's initial expression of interest.

Investment banker engagement. Filing confirms GHF was retained. Pipeline captures this via advisor actor (advisor_bmo_capital_markets) but does not extract an IB-engagement event. This is a process/advisor row, not a bid event.

Filing says GHF and management met five strategic buyers including G&W at the 2016 Connections Convention (April 3-6, 2016). Alex date of 2016-04-13 may represent a different contact or follow-up. Pipeline does not capture G&W's initial contact as a separate bidder_interest event, only later events.

Filing says 'In early July 2016, a potential strategic buyer (Party C) approached the Company's management'. Party C was a late entrant who executed a confidentiality agreement separately. Pipeline does not extract a separate NDA event for Party C.

Party A is mentioned in the filing as one of 11 strategic NDA signers but never submitted a bid. The filing says 'One strategic buyer and one financial buyer elected not to submit an LOI.' Party A's drop is inferred since they never bid. Pipeline does not have a drop event for Party A.
Filing says 'One strategic buyer and one financial buyer elected not to submit an LOI.' This anonymous financial buyer drop is captured by Alex. Pipeline does not extract this.

Filing says 'On July 26, 2016, in response to feedback from representatives of GHF indicating its price and CVR structure were not competitive, G&W submitted a revised LOI, which increased its offer to $22.15 per share (including $21.02 in cash and $1.13 CVR).' Pipeline does NOT extract this revised LOI as a separate event. This is a pipeline extraction gap.

Filing says 'Party E withdrew its revised proposal on August 2, 2016 (but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share)'. Alex records this as a separate bid confirmation event. Pipeline does not extract this withdrawal-plus-confirmation as a separate event.

Alex records Party B's bid as 'Executed' at $24, meaning their LOI terms were confirmed after DD and became the draft merger agreement terms. Pipeline does not have a separate 'executed' event for Party B -- only for G&W. Party B's bid was being negotiated up to August 12 when G&W's higher bid overtook it.
Filing says 'Party B indicated that it would not increase its price.' Alex records this as a drop on 2016-08-12. Pipeline does not extract a drop event for Party B. This is a pipeline extraction gap.

aggregate_rows.pipeline_counterpart: evt_002 (NDA with Party A + grouped actors)
aggregate_rows.note: Pipeline extracts count_assertions: 11 strategic NDA signers, 14 financial NDA signers = 25. Matches Alex.

Filing says 'the Company received nine written indications of interest (IOIs) from potential buyers, with offer prices per share ranging from $17.93 to $26.50'. Pipeline does not extract the IOI round as a batch event. The IOIs are a distinct stage from the later LOIs. Pipeline skips this round entirely and jumps to Party C's July 12 IOI and the late-July LOI batch.
Filing says 25 NDA signers minus 9 IOI submitters = 16 who dropped before IOI stage. Alex records this as a cohort drop. Pipeline does not extract this implicit dropout cohort.

Filing says 'the Transaction Committee concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from that process.' Alex records this target-initiated exclusion of 2 IOI bidders. Pipeline does not extract this.

alex_process_rows_not_matched.note: Alex marks the LOI deadline as an informal final-round boundary. Pipeline does not have a separate final-round event here; the LOI deadline was already captured in evt_004 (mid-June announcement with July 20 deadline).
Alex marks the July 27 committee decision to narrow to G&W and Party B as a formal round announcement. Pipeline does not have this as a separate event.
Target-initiated drop of Party E from the post-LOI narrowing. Pipeline does not extract separate DropTarget events for the July 27 narrowing.
Target-initiated drop of Party D from the post-LOI narrowing.
Target-initiated drop of Party C from the post-LOI narrowing.
Target-initiated drop of Party F from the post-LOI narrowing.
Party E/F final drop. Pipeline does not record this.
Alex marks the final-round close when G&W's offer was accepted.

key_pipeline_gaps: Missing G&W $22.15 revised LOI (July 26, 2016) -- filing-grounded event not extracted
Missing Party B drop (August 12, 2016) -- Party B refused to increase price
Missing Party A drop (never bid, inferred from 'one strategic buyer elected not to submit an LOI')
Missing IOI-round aggregate event (9 IOIs, May 19 - June 1, 2016)
Missing DropTarget events for July 27 narrowing (Party C, D, E, F excluded by Transaction Committee)
bid_type enrichment classifies all proposals as Informal, missing formal signals on G&W $25 revised LOI
key_alex_errors: Party F bidder_type coded as F (financial) -- filing explicitly says 'a strategic buyer'
G&W $21.15 LOI coded as all_cash=1 -- filing shows $20.02 cash + $1.13 CVR (not all-cash)
Late-July LOI dates pinned to deadline 2016-07-20 -- filing says 'In late July 2016'
